title: P0: List/serve affirmations + serve root endpoints
description: Goal: GET /affirmations (list), GET /affirmations/{id}, GET /affirmations/{id}/root (serve root audio blob). Files: backend/sharedvoice/routers/affirmations.py. Steps (TDD). Acceptance: endpoints return the seeded corpus and serve a root blob.

title: P0: Root upload endpoint + 48k-mono ingest
description: Goal: curator uploads a root recording; ingest resamples to canonical 48k mono, stores blob + Recording row. Files: backend/sharedvoice/routers/roots.py, backend/sharedvoice/audio/ingest.py. Steps (TDD): upload a non-48k clip -> asserted stored at 48k mono, duration computed. Acceptance: test green.

title: P0: Frontend shell — list affirmations, play root via <audio>
description: Goal: minimal framework-light TS frontend (Vite + vanilla TS) that lists affirmations from the API and plays the root through an HTML audio element — NOT a Web Audio buffer source (iOS mute-switch safety). Files: frontend/ (vite+ts), index.html, src/main.ts. Steps: 1. pnpm scaffold 2. fetch /affirmations + render list 3. play root via audio element. Acceptance: pnpm dev shows the list and plays a root.

title: P0: FastAPI app skeleton + /health
description: Goal: minimal FastAPI app boots with a healthcheck. Files: backend/sharedvoice/app.py, backend/sharedvoice/main.py. Steps (TDD): 1. test GET /health returns 200 2. implement app factory + /health 3. run uvicorn. Acceptance: pytest green; /health serves.

title: P0: Storage interface (FS blobs + SQLite metadata)
description: Goal: storage behind an INTERFACE so it can move to object storage later without touching the pipeline. Files: backend/sharedvoice/storage/base.py (Storage protocol), backend/sharedvoice/storage/local.py (filesystem + SQLite impl). Steps (TDD): 1. define protocol (put_blob/get_blob/path + metadata CRUD) 2. test local impl round-trips blob + metadata 3. implement. Acceptance: tests green; audio pipeline never imports the concrete impl directly.

title: P0: Data model — Affirmation + Recording (root)
description: Goal: SQLite schema + models. Affirmation(id,title,body_text,order,set,root_recording_id); Recording(id,affirmation_id,audio_path,sample_rate,duration,phrase_boundaries?). Files: backend/sharedvoice/models.py. Steps (TDD): create+read round-trip. Acceptance: tests green. Take + MixRequest models come in P1/P3.

title: P0: Seed affirmation corpus as a data file (titles + placeholder bodies)
description: Goal: load the 16 affirmations as DATA, NOT hardcoded (Tarpa's real liturgy body text arrives later from Frank). Files: data/affirmations.yaml. Content: 16 titles with order + morning/evening set tags; body_text = placeholder for now. Titles: Waking; Morning Community; Affirmation of My Path; Motivational; The Five Remembrances; Mental Discipline; Abiding on the Breath; Altruistic; Four Gifts; The Four Steps of Acceptance; Appreciation; The Four Expressions of Softness; Affirmation to Shine; Refuge Pledge; The Four Noble Truths; Evening. Steps: author data file + loader test. Acceptance: loader yields 16; morning/evening sets resolve.

title: P0: Nix flake dev shell (python+audio+ffmpeg+node/pnpm)
description: Goal: reproducible 'nix develop' shell. Files: flake.nix, flake.lock. Provides python3 + numpy/scipy/librosa/soundfile, ffmpeg, node + pnpm. Steps: 1. write flake.nix devShell 2. verify nix develop runs python import of numpy,scipy,librosa,soundfile 3. verify ffmpeg and pnpm on PATH; pytest + ruff runnable. Acceptance: nix develop drops into a shell with all tools present.

title: SharedVoice — async murmur-choir for a sangha
description:
Self-hosted web app: a remote Buddhist sangha (SBT, secular) recites daily affirmations together ASYNCHRONOUSLY. Tarpa records a ROOT recitation; others record reciting along on headphones; the server ALIGNS each take to the root; a curator picks voices and downloads a MIX — the felt result is 'a hall full of people reciting together' though nobody was online at once. Replaces live Zoom (round-trip latency kills sync). Domain sharedvoice.us; private, non-commercial, one community; audio is sensitive by default.

THE design fact: recitation NOT chant -> sharp consonant transients -> (1) alignment is EASY (windowed cross-correlation, NO DTW/phase-vocoder/time-stretch EVER) and (2) the aesthetic target is a MURMUR (phrase-start spread in [80,250]ms, AVOID the [20,60]ms flam valley), not tight unison.

Phases: P0 scaffold -> P1 MVP loop (global-offset align) -> P2 per-phrase + murmur -> P3 curation/polish. Full canonical spec: MemPalace sharedvoice/decisions drawer f7c9d745 (Frank's build brief, 2026-07-02).

title: P1 (stub): WAV download — closes the MVP loop end to end
description: Stub: WAV download of the mix; closes the whole MVP loop (gate -> capture -> upload -> global align -> sum-mix -> WAV). Detail later.

title: P1 (stub): upload take + 48k-mono ingest + Take row
description: Stub: upload a take, ingest to 48k mono, create a Take row (id,affirmation_id,contributor_name,audio_path,alignment,created_at). Detail later.

title: P1 (stub): global-offset aligner (windowed xcorr on onset env, +/-1s guard)
description: Stub: global-offset aligner -- windowed cross-correlation on an onset-strength envelope, +/-1s search window (anaphora guard). Satisfies the offset-recovery + anaphora tests. Detail later.

title: P1 (stub): sum-mix + peak-normalize + soft limiter
description: Stub: sum aligned tracks (numpy) + peak-normalize + soft limiter (tanh or lookahead) so the mix never clips as voices stack. Satisfies mix no-clip invariant. Detail later.

title: P1 test: mix invariants (no-clip, mute=exact-remove, spread distribution)
description: Goal: pin the mix engine contracts. Files: backend/tests/test_mix.py; contract for backend/sharedvoice/audio/mix.py. Assert: (a) an N-track sum never clips after the soft limiter, (b) muting a take removes EXACTLY its contribution, (c) spread=0 collapses toward unison and higher spread widens the phrase-start offset DISTRIBUTION while AVOIDING the [20,60]ms flam band -- assert the distribution, not just a mean. TDD RED first.

title: P1 (stub): headphone gate before recording
description: Stub: kind 'please use headphones' gate shown before record (echo cancellation is off, so speaker bleeds into mic). Detail when P0 frontend lands.

title: P1 (stub): AudioWorklet Float32 PCM capture (3 processors OFF)
description: Stub: capture raw PCM via AudioWorklet (NOT MediaRecorder). getUserMedia audio flags echoCancellation/autoGainControl/noiseSuppression all false, channelCount 1. AudioContext resumed from the record-tap gesture. Detail later.

title: P1 test: anaphora regression (window must not lock wrong repetition)
description: Goal: guard the REAL anaphora bug. The liturgy repeats line openings ('I will no longer allow my' x5, 'May all be', 'I appreciate') so an UNCONSTRAINED cross-correlation can lock onto the wrong repetition and shift a take by a whole line. Files: backend/tests/test_align_anaphora.py. Steps: 1. build a synthetic signal with a repeated phrase at line spacing 2. assert the windowed search stays within +/-1s and recovers the TRUE offset, not the wrong repeat. Acceptance: the test FAILS if the search window is removed or widened past the guard.

title: P1 test: synthetic offset-recovery for the global aligner
description: Goal: synthetic ground-truth test that the global-offset aligner recovers known shifts. Files: backend/tests/test_align_offset.py; defines the contract for backend/sharedvoice/audio/align.py. Steps: 1. generate a reference clip; produce copies shifted by known offsets (including near the +/-1s window edges) with added noise + level changes 2. assert recovered offset within a tight sample tolerance. Method: windowed cross-correlation on an onset-strength envelope; +/-1s search window. TDD: RED first (defines the align contract).

title: Author project CLAUDE.md for sharedvoice
description: Audit item 6 (MISS). Write a project CLAUDE.md (<=200 lines) describing sharedvoice's purpose, structure, and dev commands. Deferred from /loom-adopt P1.

title: Author sharedvoice project constitution prose body
description: Fill the [HUMAN AUTHOR] TODO stubs in .claude/project-constitution.md (Tooling choices / Forbidden patterns / Bypass patterns / Lineage). Front-matter was auto-captured by /loom-adopt P5; the prose is human-authored per loom-d50.
close_reason: Filled constitution prose (mission, tooling rationale, architectural DO-NOTs, lineage) reconciled to build brief f7c9d745. Front-matter shell.enter deferred to sv-lds.1 when flake.nix lands.

title: Phase 2: per-phrase offsets + murmur controller
description: Phase 2 -- silence-split the ROOT into phrases (energy/VAD); per-phrase windowed cross-correlation; shift each phrase AS A WHOLE (NEVER stretch) with boundary crossfades. The 'spread' parameter places phrase-start offsets into [80,250]ms across contributors, AVOIDING the [20,60]ms flam valley, plus bounded per-contributor per-phrase jitter when everyone tracked Tarpa too tightly. Spend precision on list ordinals. Full spec: drawer f7c9d745. Break into detailed beads after P0/P1 land.

title: Phase 3: curation UI, sets, root-level, encoding, self-delete
description: Phase 3 -- voice-selection mixer UI (per-take toggles/faders), morning/evening set composition, adjustable + mutable root level, Opus/MP3 encoding, contributor self-delete. OPEN DESIGN QUESTIONS at this phase: (1) identity for self-delete -- no accounts, so a per-take secret (unguessable delete link is the lean); contributor_name is free text, not identity. (2) curator vs participant access on a private self-hosted deploy. Full spec: drawer f7c9d745. Break into detailed beads later.

title: Backfill 7 DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME sentinels in docs/
description: P3 docs-scaffold left 7 DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME sentinels (docs/index.md, how-to/install.md x2, tutorials/getting-started.md, explanation/mental-model.md, mkdocs.yml x2). 'mkdocs build --strict' fails until all are replaced. grep -r DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME docs/ mkdocs.yml
